software_engineer.answer: Dynamic programming with 2D table. dp[i][j] = min operations. Time: O(m*n), Space: O(min(m,n)) optimized.

software_engineer.text: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
software_engineer.answer: Sliding window with hash set. Time: O(n), Space: O(min(m,n)).

software_engineer.text: Design and implement an LRU (Least Recently Used) Cache with get and put operations in O(1) time.
software_engineer.answer: Hash map + Doubly Linked List. Both operations O(1).

software_engineer.text: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map, compute how much water it can trap after raining.
software_engineer.answer: Two pointers or precompute max left/right. Time: O(n), Space: O(1) or O(n).

software_engineer.text: Implement in-order, pre-order, and post-order traversal of a binary tree iteratively.
software_engineer.answer: Use stack for iterative. Time: O(n), Space: O(h) where h is height.

software_engineer.text: Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.
software_engineer.answer: Sort by start time, then merge. Time: O(n log n), Space: O(n).

software_engineer.text: Given an array of integers and a target sum, find two numbers that add up to the target. Return their indices.
software_engineer.answer: Use hash map to store complement. Time: O(n), Space: O(n).

software_engineer.text: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
software_engineer.answer: Min-heap of size k or quickselect. Time: O(n log k) or O(n) avg.

software_engineer.text: Explain the TLS handshake process in detail.
software_engineer.answer: 1) ClientHello: Client sends supported cipher suites, TLS version, random. 2) ServerHello: Server chooses cipher, sends certificate (public key), ServerKeyExchange, ServerHelloDone. 3) Client verifies certificate, generates PreMasterSecret, encrypts with server's public key, sends ClientKeyExchange. 4) Both generate session keys from PreMasterSecret + randoms. 5) ChangeCipherSpec + Finished (encrypted). 6) Server responds with Finished. Asymmetric crypto for key exchange, symmetric for data encryption.

software_engineer.text: Compare microservices and monolithic architectures. What are the trade-offs?
software_engineer.answer: Monolith: Single deployable unit, simple dev, tight coupling, harder to scale independently, one tech stack. Microservices: Independent deployment, service boundaries, tech diversity, independent scaling, but distributed complexity, network latency, data consistency challenges (eventual consistency), operational overhead. Choose based on team size, domain complexity, scale needs.

software_engineer.text: Given a string and a dictionary of words, determine if the string can be segmented into space-separated dictionary words.
software_engineer.answer: Dynamic programming. dp[i] = True if s[0:i] can be segmented. Time: O(n^2), Space: O(n).

software_engineer.text: Explain consistent hashing and why it's used in distributed systems.
software_engineer.answer: Consistent hashing maps both nodes and keys to a ring. When a node is added/removed, only keys near that node need remapping (K/n keys on average). Used in load balancing, distributed caching (memcached), and databases (Cassandra, DynamoDB). Benefits: Minimal reorganization, scalability, fault tolerance.

software_engineer.text: Explain how garbage collection works in managed languages like Java or Go.
software_engineer.answer: GC automatically reclaims memory no longer referenced. Mark-and-sweep: Mark reachable objects, sweep unmarked. Reference counting: Count references, free when 0 (circular reference problem). Generational GC: Young generation (frequent, fast minor GC), old generation (full GC less frequent). Algorithms: Serial, Parallel, CMS (concurrent mark sweep), G1 (region-based). Trade-offs: Throughput vs pause time.

software_engineer.text: How do hash tables handle collisions? Explain separate chaining vs open addressing with trade-offs.
software_engineer.answer: Chaining: Each bucket is a linked list. Simple, handles any load factor, but uses extra space. Open addressing: Find next slot via probing (linear, quadratic, double hash). Better cache locality, but suffers from clustering, needs lower load factor (< 0.75).

software_engineer.text: Design a data structure that supports adding numbers and finding the median of all numbers.
software_engineer.answer: Two heaps - max heap for lower half, min heap for upper half. Time: O(log n) add, O(1) median.

software_engineer.text: What are REST API design best practices? Explain idempotency and its importance.
software_engineer.answer: REST: Stateless, cacheable, uniform interface (URIs), layered. Best practices: Proper HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), status codes, versioning (/v1/), pagination, HATEOAS. Idempotency: Same result for repeated calls. GET, PUT, DELETE should be idempotent. Important for retries in distributed systems, prevents duplicate side effects.

software_engineer.text: Compare BFS and DFS for graph traversal. When is one preferred over the other?
software_engineer.answer: BFS: Uses queue, explores level by level, finds shortest path in unweighted graphs, more memory O(w) where w is max width. DFS: Uses stack/recursion, explores deep first, uses O(h) memory where h is height, can get stuck in infinite paths. BFS for shortest path, DFS for cycle detection, topological sort, connected components.

software_engineer.text: Given a string containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
software_engineer.answer: Use stack. Push opening, pop and match closing. Time: O(n), Space: O(n).

software_engineer.text: There are courses labeled 0 to n-1. Some courses have prerequisites. Determine if you can finish all courses.
software_engineer.answer: Topological sort using Kahn's algorithm or DFS cycle detection. Time: O(V+E).

software_engineer.text: What are different load balancing algorithms? When would you use each?
software_engineer.answer: Round Robin: Simple, equal distribution, doesn't consider load. Least Connections: Sends to server with fewest active connections, good for varying request times. IP Hash: Same client always to same server (session affinity). Weighted: Some servers get more traffic (heterogeneous capacity). Random: Simple, sometimes used with health checks. Consistent Hashing: For distributed caches/databases. Layer 4 (transport) vs Layer 7 (application) load balancing.

frontend_developer.text: Explain the CAP theorem. What are CP and AP systems with examples?
frontend_developer.answer: CAP: Consistency (all nodes see same data), Availability (every request gets response), Partition Tolerance (system works despite network partitions). In distributed systems, you can only guarantee 2 of 3. CP (Consistency + Partition Tolerance): MongoDB, HBase, Zookeeper - consistent during partitions. AP (Availability + Partition Tolerance): Cassandra, DynamoDB, CouchDB - available but may return stale data. No distributed system can be CA.

frontend_developer.text: What is Dynamic Programming? Explain the two main approaches: memoization vs tabulation.

frontend_developer.text: How do database indexes work internally? Compare B-Tree vs Hash indexes.

B-Tree better for range queries, sorting. Hash better for exact match. Clustered index stores data in order, non-clustered stores pointers.

frontend_developer.text: Explain how the event loop works in Node.js or browser JavaScript.
frontend_developer.answer: Event loop enables single-threaded async execution. Components: Call Stack (sync code), Web APIs (async operations), Callback Queue (microtasks - Promises, macrotasks - setTimeout). This allows non-blocking I/O despite single thread. setTimeout(fn, 0) defers to next tick.

devops_engineer.text: Explain the TLS handshake process in detail.
devops_engineer.answer: 1) ClientHello: Client sends supported cipher suites, TLS version, random. 2) ServerHello: Server chooses cipher, sends certificate (public key), ServerKeyExchange, ServerHelloDone. 3) Client verifies certificate, generates PreMasterSecret, encrypts with server's public key, sends ClientKeyExchange. 4) Both generate session keys from PreMasterSecret + randoms. 5) ChangeCipherSpec + Finished (encrypted). 6) Server responds with Finished. product_designer.text: Explain the TLS handshake process in detail.
product_designer.answer: 1) ClientHello: Client sends supported cipher suites, TLS version, random. 2) ServerHello: Server chooses cipher, sends certificate (public key), ServerKeyExchange, ServerHelloDone. 3) Client verifies certificate, generates PreMasterSecret, encrypts with server's public key, sends ClientKeyExchange. 4) Both generate session keys from PreMasterSecret + randoms. 5) ChangeCipherSpec + Finished (encrypted). 6) Server responds with Finished. Asymmetric crypto for key exchange, symmetric for data encryption.
